channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. and Iran trade fire amid month-old ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. says it intercepted Iranian attacks on three Navy ships in Strait of Hormuz, putting a strain on the month-old ceasefire between the two countries.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. says it intercepted Iranian attacks on three Navy ships in Strait of Hormuz, putting a strain on the month-old ceasefire between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Rep. Adam Smith, D-Wash., on the latest round of strikes between the U.S. and Iran
item.description: Is the U.S. moving closer to ending the war with Iran? NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Democratic Rep. Adam Smith of Washington, ranking member of the House Armed Services Committee.
item.content:encoded: Is the U.S. moving closer to ending the war with Iran? NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Democratic Rep. Adam Smith of Washington, ranking member of the House Armed Services Committee.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: David Attenborough celebrates his 100th birthday
item.description: He was born before the Great Depression, came of age in WWII, and is still making wildlife documentaries. Brits call David Attenborough a national hero, as he celebrates his 100th birthday.
item.content:encoded: He was born before the Great Depression, came of age in WWII, and is still making wildlife documentaries. Brits call David Attenborough a national hero, as he celebrates his 100th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Trump's Truth Social lays bare narrow obsessions of an extremely online president
item.description: The bulk of the president's social media posts don't make news. But taken together they show what's on his mind as he leads the nation through war and domestic turmoil.
item.content:encoded:
The bulk of the president's social media posts don't make news. But taken together they show what's on his mind as he leads the nation through war and domestic turmoil.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. and Iran trade fire amid month-old ceasefire, Tennessee becomes first state to draw new U.S. House map after SCOTUS decision, U.S.'s continued strikes on alleged drug boats raise questions.
item.content:encoded: U.S. and Iran trade fire amid month-old ceasefire, Tennessee becomes first state to draw new U.S. House map after SCOTUS decision, U.S.'s continued strikes on alleged drug boats raise questions.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Linda Perry talks about her new album, 'Let it die here'
item.description: Linda Perry wrote a series of hits in the early part of the 2000s, like Christina Aguilera's "Beautiful." Now, Perry's releasing her own album called "Let It Die Here."
item.content:encoded: Linda Perry wrote a series of hits in the early part of the 2000s, like Christina Aguilera's "Beautiful." Now, Perry's releasing her own album called "Let It Die Here."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Authorities track dozens of passengers who left cruise ship with hantavirus outbreak
item.description: Authorities are monitoring passengers who disembarked a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship while tracking down people who came in contact with them.
item.content:encoded: Authorities are monitoring passengers who disembarked a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship while tracking down people who came in contact with them.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: As war on Ukraine continues, Russia prepares for smaller Victory Day parade
item.description: Russia's annual Victory Day parade will be smaller than previous ones as the effects of the war on Ukraine take a toll.
item.content:encoded: Russia's annual Victory Day parade will be smaller than previous ones as the effects of the war on Ukraine take a toll.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Democrats want to raise taxes on the wealthy. A look at the math
item.description: Democrats want to tax the rich to cut taxes on worker incomes. Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen of Maryland explains and a tax policy expert shares his reservations with NPR's Planet Money team.
item.content:encoded: Democrats want to tax the rich to cut taxes on worker incomes. Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen of Maryland explains and a tax policy expert shares his reservations with NPR's Planet Money team.
item.dc:creator: Darian Woods

item.title: Some people in Iran have kept internet access despite government-imposed blackout
item.description: Iran is experiencing the longest internet blackout ever recorded with 99% of the population offline. But certain people — with "white internet" — have stayed connected this entire time.
item.content:encoded: Iran is experiencing the longest internet blackout ever recorded with 99% of the population offline. But certain people — with "white internet" — have stayed connected this entire time.
item.dc:creator: Arezou Rezvani
